verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted dimeric glycoprotein belonging to the PDGF/VEGF growth factor family. It is synthesized with a signal peptide, processed through the classical secretory pathway, and released as a soluble disulfide-linked homodimer into the extracellular space. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI signal, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Pre-`no` checklist: (1) Anti-VEGFA antibodies (bevacizumab, ranibizumab) target the circulating/soluble pool, not cell-surface-anchored protein — these are anti-soluble-ligand programs. (2) No TM precursor stage — VEGFA is not a single-pass TM proligand; it is constitutively secreted. (3) Heparin-binding to ECM/HSPG is reversible non-covalent equilibrium — not wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. (4) No documented minority PM pool via surface biotinylation or flow cytometry on intact cells. (5) No gene-specific evidence for cancer ecto-display, cell-death-induced surface exposure, germline-restricted surface display, or activation-induced translocation. It is a canonical secreted growth factor with no stable outer-leaflet anchor.
